name: Atlanta to Orlando LTL
hero-headline: Atlanta, GA to Orlando, FL LTL Freight
subheadline: 474 miles. 2-3 business day transit. 11 carriers active on this lane.
body-content:
Lane Overview: Atlanta to Orlando

The Atlanta to Orlando LTL lane covers approximately 474 miles between the Southeast and Southeast regions. Standard transit runs 2-3 business days depending on carrier routing and terminal schedules. Common equipment on this corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Atlanta Hub, Charlotte Terminal, Memphis Freight Hub support consolidation and transit optimization. Hurricane risk Jun-Nov. Consistent retail demand year-round.

How WARP Operates the Atlanta to Orlando Lane

WARP covers the Atlanta to Orlando corridor with 11 vetted carriers providing LTL capacity.

  • Service capabilities on this lane include: Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery; Estimated 2-3 business day transit window; Pallet-level tracking with delivery appointment scheduling.
  • Shipments receive pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery with appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation.
  • Nearby cross-dock operations at Atlanta Hub and Charlotte Terminal enable consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur.
  • Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change.
  • WARP's operations team manages carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution without requiring shipper follow-up.

Operating Details

Key operational considerations for this lane:

  • Estimated lane distance: 474 miles (Atlanta to Orlando)
  • Transit window: 2-3 business days for standard LTL service
  • Available equipment: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled)
  • Active carriers on this lane: 11
  • Nearest cross-dock facilities: Atlanta Hub, Charlotte Terminal, Memphis Freight Hub, Miami Gateway
  • Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery milestones
  • Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes with automated escalation
  • Delivery appointment scheduling and confirmation managed by WARP operations

Pricing & Rate Factors

Estimated LTL rates on the Atlanta to Orlando lane range from $1,525 to $2,033, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand.

  • Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and whether accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup apply.
  • At 474 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $3.
  • 75/mile).
  • Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices.
  • These are modeled estimates.

Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Atlanta to Orlando LTL (474-mile corridor; 2–3 day transit window; 11 vetted carriers active; cross-dock access at Atlanta Hub and Charlotte Terminal). Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception trends before scaling volume. Start with a single shipment to benchmark WARP against your current process.

faq-schema:
How much does LTL freight from Atlanta, GA to Orlando, FL cost?

Estimated LTL rates range from $1,525 to $2,033 based on freight class, weight, and pallet count. Actual rates depend on shipment specifics and current market conditions. Get an instant quote for real-time pricing.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Atlanta to Orlando?

Standard LTL transit on this 474-mile lane is 2-3 business days. Actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Atlanta to Orlando lane?

WARP maintains a vetted network of approximately 11 active carriers on this corridor. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Atlanta to Orlando in real time?

Yes. Every shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Atlanta to Orlando corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.
